prevalence.trend: increasing in some urban contexts; polysubstance use concerns

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: impaired control, social impairment, risky use, tolerance/withdrawal (substance-specific); severity by criteria count

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited; case-based identification in APS/CAPS AD
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; relapse common without long-term support

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS screening variable; specialized care in CAPS AD and inpatient detox units where available
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Psychosocial interventions, harm reduction, and medication-assisted treatment where applicable (e.g., alcohol); comorbidity management
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma, uneven CAPS AD coverage, and limited long-term rehabilitation and housing support
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: CAPS AD network and growing harm reduction initiatives in some municipalities
